results.title: A Bi-modal Dataset for Robust Robotic Depth Perception in ... - arXiv
results.content:
## IV Experiments

We used the ShotcreteDepth dataset ourselves to test various stereo, depth completion, and depth estimation baselines. The goal of these experiments was to elucidate the characteristics of the dataset.

### IV-A Experimental Setup [...] We introduce ShotcreteDepth, a bi-modal dataset from the construction domain
that captures both an active shotcreting process and general construction
environments. The dataset comprises stereo RGB imagery and LiDAR point
clouds acquired under harsh real-world conditions, including high turbidity
and poor illumination. Such conditions adversely affect sensor measurements,
leading to incomplete and noisy observations that pose significant challenges
for perception systems in autonomous applications. Alongside the dataset,
we release a lightweight annotation tool designed for time-efficient labeling of
LiDAR point clouds. ShotcreteDepth consists of 11,252 temporally synchronized
data samples, of which 220 are annotated for evaluation purposes. The dataset [...] Stereo matching is the task of finding correspondences between images captured by a stereo camera
allowing to estimate depth of the observed scene. While Semi-Global Matching by
Hirschmüller  became an industrial standard due to its favorable accuracy-efficiency
trade-off , the deep learning methods have dominated the task. Some of the methods aggregate
cost volume [31, 52, 53, 64], which is often memory expensive. Other methods
perform recurrent disparity refinement [40, 36, 8, 63],
which is less time-efficient. An alternative approach avoiding the construction of a cost-volume
is utilizing attention mechanisms establishing correspondences between stereo
images . The most recent methods combine the mentioned
approaches [4, 26, 60] and simultaneously

results.title: Tunnel Construction Methods: TBM vs. NATM
results.content:
Advantages of NATM Tunnels

1. Adaptability to Variable Ground Conditions

NATM is highly flexible and can accommodate changing geological conditions during construction.

2. Reduced Equipment Requirements

Compared to TBM systems, NATM generally requires less specialized machinery and lower mobilization costs.

3. Suitability for Complex Tunnel Geometries

The method is well suited for noncircular tunnel shapes, cross-passages, and underground caverns.

4. Cost Efficiency for Smaller Projects

For shorter tunnels or uncertain ground conditions, NATM can provide a more economical alternative.

Limitations of NATM Tunnels

NATM construction may involve:

results.content: shall be clean and free from any substances that cause discoloration of the shotcrete where the finished surface will be exposed to view. Except as otherwise specified in Section 17d of this specification and except for surfaces to which additional shotcrete is to be applied, shotcrete may be coated with curing compound as an alternative to the continued application of moisture. 33 – 10 The compound shall be sprayed on the moist shotcrete surface as soon as rebound has been removed and any required repairs are completed, or as soon as water curing is discontinued. The curing compound shall be thoroughly mixed immediately before applied and continuously agitated during application. It shall be applied at a uniform rate of not less than 1 gallon per 100 square feet of surface for natural

# Performance of Single-Layer Lining Using Shotcrete and Reinforcement Ribs Employed for Supporting Large-Span Tunnel

### Zijian Li

### Pinxin Diao

### Weihua Lu

### Yongxing Zhang

Correspondence: zhanguongxing81@aliyun.com

#### Roles

Received 2023 Oct 7; Revised 2023 Dec 7; Accepted 2023 Dec 9; Collection date 2023 Dec.

Licensee MDPI, Basel, Switzerland. This article is an open access article distributed under the terms and conditions of the Creative Commons Attribution (CC BY) license ().

## Abstract [...] Single-layer tunnel lining using shotcrete has the significant advantages of reducing the tunnel excavation volume and saving construction materials, which has been gradually applied in tunnel construction. The high-performance concretes are generally adopted in single-layer tunnel lining for enhancing the bearing capacity of the tunnel lining, whereas the single-layer tunnel lining may still induce damages due to the adverse conditions such as shallow buried depth of the tunnel, and further study related to its application condition is thus required. This paper presents a study of the single-layer tunnel lining with shotcrete employed for supporting the large-span tunnel, in which the reinforcement ribs are also adopted in the single-layer lining for improving the lining stiffness and

results.title: Depth Indicators - Stratacrete
results.content:
Skip to content

Stratacrete Logo

Stratacrete Logo

###### Shotcrete Plus

# Depth Indicators

Depth Indicatorsdevteam2024-12-20T15:52:04+10:00

###### Shotcrete Plus

## Our Depth Indicator System

The Shotcrete Plus ‘Depth Indicator System’ has evolved with success at Stratacrete’s major sites.

As an example, Stratacrete installed 1200 indicators during October at the Cadia Deeps operation in Orange. As the system is the only pre-application depth gauge it has had a positive impact on both the efficiency of cubes / face, as well as improved operator proficiency.

Shotcrete Depth Indicators (SDI) are used to assist with the control of shotcrete thickness whilst applying sprayed concrete. They are designed to be installed without placing personnel beneath unsupported ground. [...] ###### Depth Indicator

## Thickness Requirement



Figure 1  
 Depth Indicator



Figure 2  
 25mm Extension



Figure 3  
 Complete set including washer & cone

If a thickness of 50mm is required refer to Figure 1. The measurement from the base of the SDI to the top is 50mm. If 75mm thickness is required, refer to Figure 2. Place the yellow extension on top of the base unit and this will provide the added thickness.

###### Depth Indicator

## Application Method



Figure 4  
 ISR 70-05 Simson Glue



Figure 5  
 37cm Barrel Gun Suitable for Propak dispensing [...] Figure 5  
 37cm Barrel Gun Suitable for Propak dispensing

If spraying a depth of 75mm. The washer is attached to the top of the indicator and then the cone is placed on the end. The depth indicator is then inserted into the tip of the shotcrete nozzle which is held by the shotcrete boom.

Simson ISR 70-05 Adhesive (our preferred adhesive) is then applied to the base of the depth indicator. The nozzle tip that has the indicator and adhesive applied is then placed onto the required substrate prior to shotcrete application. The washer and cone has been uniquely designed so that when the shotcrete is applied, the cone protectors the washer from the percussion of the blast which will then leave the washer as the clearly visible guide to the 75mm mark.
